hero.title: Next-Generation Learning Platform
hero.subtitle: Learn programming, web development, game creation and Data Science from the best instructors at your own pace
catalog.title: Course Catalog
catalog.subtitle: Find the course that's right for you
footer.aboutDesc: Maestria is an educational platform by Maestro7IT for learning programming, web development, game creation and Data Science. Learn from the best instructors at your own pace.
footer.contribution: User content is available under CC BY-SA 4.0 license with attribution required
legal.personalDataConsent: Consent to Processing of Personal Data
legal.head: Director:
terms.s1_1: These Terms of Service (hereinafter — the «Agreement») regulate the relations between Maestro7IT, represented by Director Dupley Maxim Igorevich (hereinafter — the «Administration»), and the user of the Maestria educational platform (hereinafter — the «User»).
terms.s1_2: Using the Maestria platform means the User's unconditional acceptance of this Agreement and the conditions specified herein.
terms.s1_3: The Administration reserves the right to change, supplement, or update this Agreement at any time without prior notice. Continued use of the platform after changes constitutes acceptance of the new version of the Agreement.
terms.s1_4: The Maestria platform is designed to provide educational services in programming, web development, game creation, Data Science, and other areas.
terms.s2_1: To fully use the platform's functionality, the User must complete the registration procedure by providing accurate and complete data.
terms.s2_2: The User is responsible for the accuracy of the information provided and undertakes to update it in case of changes.
terms.s2_3: The User has no right to transfer their credentials to third parties and is responsible for all actions performed using their account.
terms.s2_4: The Administration has the right to block the User's account in case of violation of this Agreement or provision of inaccurate data.
terms.s3_1: The platform provides the User with access to educational courses, study materials, tests, and interactive assignments.
terms.s3_2: The User undertakes to use the platform exclusively for educational purposes and not to violate the rights of other users and copyright holders.
terms.s3_3: It is prohibited to: distribute platform materials without Administration's permission; use automated means to access content; disrupt the platform's functionality; create multiple accounts.
terms.s3_4: Free courses are provided under an open license and may be used for personal educational purposes.
terms.s3_5: Paid courses are provided after payment in accordance with the selected plan. Refund conditions are determined by the Administration's refund policy.
terms.s4_1: All platform materials (courses, lessons, tests, images, interface source code) are objects of intellectual property of the Administration or course authors.
terms.s4_2: Copying, distribution, modification, and use of platform materials without the copyright holder's written consent are prohibited, except as expressly provided by this Agreement or the legislation of the Russian Federation.
terms.s4_3: The name «Maestria», logo, and corporate style are trademarks of Maestro7IT.
terms.s5_1: User content created and published on the platform (problem solutions, comments, articles, etc.) is available under the Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike 4.0 International (CC BY-SA 4.0) license.
terms.s5_2: The CC BY-SA 4.0 license permits: copying and distribution of the material in any medium and format; adaptation, remixing, transformation, and creation of new materials based on this material.
terms.s5_3: When using user content, the following conditions are mandatory: attribution; indicating a link to the license; indicating changes if any were made; distributing derivative materials under the same CC BY-SA 4.0 license.
terms.s5_4: By publishing content on the platform, the User confirms that they have the right to publish it and agrees to the terms of the CC BY-SA 4.0 license.
terms.s5_5: The Administration is not responsible for user content but reserves the right to moderate and remove content that violates this Agreement.
terms.s6_1: The Administration does not guarantee uninterrupted platform operation and is not responsible for temporary technical failures.
terms.s6_2: The Administration is not responsible for any losses arising from the use or inability to use the platform.
terms.s6_3: The User bears full responsibility for the content they publish and for violating the rights of third parties.
terms.s6_4: The Administration has the right to temporarily suspend access to the platform for technical maintenance without prior notice.
terms.s7_1: All disputes arising in connection with the execution of this Agreement, the parties will seek to resolve through negotiations.
terms.s7_2: If it is impossible to resolve the dispute through negotiations, the dispute shall be resolved in court at the location of the Administration in accordance with the legislation of the Russian Federation.
terms.s7_3: Before going to court, the User is obliged to send a written claim to the Administration.
terms.s7_4: The claim review period is 30 (thirty) calendar days from the date of its receipt.
terms.s8_1: For all questions related to this Agreement, you can contact the Administration:
terms.s8_2: You can also send a letter to: maksimqwe42@mail.ru.
terms.userContentLicense: User content is available under the license
privacy.s1_1: This Privacy Policy (hereinafter — the «Policy») describes the procedure for collecting, storing, using, and protecting personal data of users of the Maestria educational platform (hereinafter — the «Platform»), owned by Maestro7IT, represented by Director Dupley Maxim Igorevich.
privacy.s1_2: By using the Platform, the User confirms their acceptance of the terms of this Policy.
privacy.s1_3: The Administration undertakes to ensure the confidentiality of the User's personal data in accordance with the legislation of the Russian Federation, including Federal Law No. 152-FZ of 27.07.2006 «On Personal Data».
privacy.s1_4: The Administration reserves the right to make changes to this Policy. The current version is always available on the Platform's page.
privacy.s2_1: The Administration collects the following categories of User data:
privacy.s2_1a: name, email address, password (stored in hashed form);
privacy.s2_1b: photo, date of birth, place of residence (provided at the User's discretion);
privacy.s2_1c: completed courses, test results, learning progress;
privacy.s2_1d: IP address, browser type, operating system, cookie data;
privacy.s2_1e: pages visited, actions on the Platform, session times.
privacy.s2_2: Data collection is based on the principles of legality, fairness, and transparency.
privacy.s2_3: The Administration does not collect data related to special categories of personal data (racial origin, political views, health status, etc.).
privacy.s3_1: The User's personal data is used for the following purposes:
privacy.s3_1a: Registration and authorization on the Platform;
privacy.s3_1f: Sending notifications about new courses and updates (with consent);
privacy.s3_2: The Administration does not transfer the User's personal data to third parties without their consent, except as required by law.
privacy.s4_1: The User's personal data is stored for the period necessary to achieve the purposes of its collection, or until the User deletes their account.
privacy.s4_2: Learning data may be stored indefinitely to enable progress restoration and certificate issuance.
privacy.s4_3: The Administration takes organizational and technical measures to protect personal data from unauthorized access, alteration, disclosure, and destruction.
privacy.s4_4: Data is stored on servers located in the Russian Federation.
privacy.s5_1: The User has the right to:
privacy.s5_1a: Receive information about the processing of their personal data;
privacy.s5_1b: Request access to their personal data;
privacy.s5_1c: Demand correction of inaccurate personal data;
privacy.s5_1e: Withdraw consent to the processing of personal data;
privacy.s5_1f: Appeal the Administration's actions to the authorized body for the protection of personal data subjects' rights.
privacy.s5_2: To exercise their rights, the User may contact the Administration using the contact details specified in section 9 of this Policy.
privacy.s5_3: The User's request review period is no more than 30 calendar days.
privacy.s6_1: The Platform uses cookies to ensure functionality, analytics, and personalization of the user experience.
privacy.s6_2a: ensure Platform functionality, authorization, and security;
privacy.s6_3: The User can manage cookies through browser settings. Disabling cookies may limit Platform functionality.
privacy.s7_1: The Platform may use third-party services for analytics, payments, and other functions. These services may collect data in accordance with their own privacy policies.
privacy.s7_2: The Administration is not responsible for the privacy policies of third-party services and recommends that the User familiarize themselves independently.
privacy.s7_3: Data transfer to third-party services is carried out only to the extent necessary for providing services, in compliance with personal data protection legislation.
privacy.s8_1: The Platform may be used by persons under 18 only with the consent of parents or legal representatives.
privacy.s8_2: The Administration does not intend to collect personal data of children without the consent of their legal representatives.
privacy.s8_3: If a fact of collecting a child's data without parental consent is discovered, the Administration undertakes to immediately delete such information.
privacy.s9_1: For all questions related to the processing of personal data, you can contact:
pdc.s1_1: This Consent to the processing of personal data (hereinafter — "Consent") is drawn up in accordance with Federal Law No. 152-FZ of July 27, 2006 "On Personal Data" and governs the procedure for processing the personal data of users of the Maestria educational platform (hereinafter — "Platform"), owned by Maestro7IT represented by Director Duplei Maksim Igorevich (hereinafter — "Operator").
pdc.s1_2: By providing their personal data during registration on the Platform, the User gives their free, informed, and conscious consent to its processing by the Operator for the purposes specified in this Consent.
pdc.s1_3: The Consent is specific, informed, and conscious. The User may withdraw this Consent at any time by sending a written notice to the Operator at maksimqwe42@mail.ru.
pdc.s1_4: Using the Platform without providing consent to the processing of personal data is not possible, as processing is necessary for the provision of educational services.
pdc.s2_1: The Operator processes the following categories of the User's personal data:
pdc.s2_1a: surname, first name, patronymic (if applicable), email address, password (stored in hashed form using cryptographic algorithms);
pdc.s2_1b: photograph, date of birth, place of residence, education information (provided by the User voluntarily);
pdc.s2_1c: list of enrolled courses, test and assignment results, learning progress, certificates;
pdc.s2_1d: IP address, browser type and version, operating system, cookie data, region information;
pdc.s2_1e: pages visited, actions on the Platform, session time and duration, referral sources.
pdc.s2_2: The collection of personal data is carried out on the principles of legality, fairness, and transparency, in compliance with the requirements of data minimization necessary to achieve the stated processing purposes.
pdc.s2_3: The Operator does not process special categories of personal data (racial origin, political opinions, philosophical beliefs, health status, intimate life) or biometric personal data.
pdc.s3_1: The processing of the User's personal data is carried out for the following purposes:
pdc.s3_1a: Registration and authentication of the User on the Platform, creation and maintenance of an account;
pdc.s3_1b: Provision of educational services, including access to courses, study materials, tests, and interactive assignments;
pdc.s3_1c: Personalization of the user experience, including adaptation of the interface and recommendation of educational content;
pdc.s3_1d: Tracking learning progress, recording results, and generating certificates;
pdc.s3_1e: Feedback and technical support, processing of User inquiries;
pdc.s3_1f: Sending notifications about new courses, updates, and promotions (subject to the User's separate consent);
pdc.s3_1g: Ensuring Platform security, preventing unauthorized access and fraud.
pdc.s3_2: The Operator does not process personal data for purposes incompatible with the purposes for which they were collected.
pdc.s4_1: The legal basis for processing personal data is the consent of the data subject (clause 1, part 1, article 6 of Federal Law No. 152-FZ).
pdc.s4_2: Processing of personal data may also be carried out for the performance of a contract to which the data subject is a party (clause 5, part 1, article 6 of Law No. 152-FZ), in particular for providing access to paid educational courses.
pdc.s4_3: In cases established by law, processing may be carried out without the subject's consent, including for the fulfillment of obligations imposed on the Operator by legislation (clause 3, part 1, article 6 of Law No. 152-FZ).
pdc.s4_4: Processing of personal data based on the legitimate interests of the Operator (clause 7, part 1, article 6 of Law No. 152-FZ) is carried out only on the condition that the rights and freedoms of the data subject do not prevail over the interests of the Operator.
pdc.s5_1: The User's personal data is processed and stored for the period necessary to achieve the purposes of its processing, but no longer than the duration of the User's account.
pdc.s5_2: Learning data (completed courses, test results, certificates) may be stored indefinitely to ensure the possibility of restoring learning progress and confirming qualifications.
pdc.s5_3: Personal data is stored on servers located in the territory of the Russian Federation, using organizational and technical protection measures provided for by legislation.
pdc.s6_1: In accordance with the legislation of the Russian Federation, the User has the following rights regarding their personal data:
pdc.s6_1a: The right to receive information about the processing of their personal data, including the legal basis, purposes, methods of processing, and retention periods;
pdc.s6_1b: The right of access to their personal data, including the right to obtain a copy of the data being processed;
pdc.s6_1c: The right to request correction of inaccurate personal data, as well as the completion of incomplete data;
pdc.s6_1d: The right to request deletion of personal data in the absence of legal grounds for continuing its processing;
pdc.s6_1e: The right to withdraw consent to the processing of personal data at any time by sending a written notice;
pdc.s6_1f: The right to appeal the actions or inaction of the Operator to the authorized body for the protection of data subjects' rights (Roskomnadzor).
pdc.s6_2: To exercise their rights, the User may contact the Operator using the contact details specified in Section 7 of this Consent.
pdc.s6_3: The period for reviewing a data subject's request shall not exceed 30 (thirty) calendar days from the date of its receipt.
pdc.s7_1: For all questions related to the processing of personal data, you may contact the Operator:
pdc.s7_2: The User also has the right to file a complaint with the Federal Service for Supervision of Communications, Information Technology, and Mass Media (Roskomnadzor) at: 123112, Moscow, Presnenskaya Embankment, 2, building 1.
offer.s1_1: This document is a public offer (hereinafter — "Offer") of Maestro7IT represented by Director Duplei Maksim Igorevich (hereinafter — "Provider"), addressed to an unlimited number of persons (hereinafter — "Customer"), in accordance with Article 437 of the Civil Code of the Russian Federation.
offer.s1_2: The Offer becomes effective from the moment of its publication on the Maestria Platform website and remains in force indefinitely until its withdrawal or modification by the Provider.
offer.s1_3: The Provider reserves the right to make changes to this Offer without prior notice to the Customer. The new version of the Offer becomes effective from the moment of its publication on the website.
offer.s1_4: Use of the Platform and payment for services after changes have been made to the Offer constitutes the Customer's agreement with the new version.
offer.s2_1: The Provider undertakes to provide the Customer with access to educational courses published on the Maestria Platform, in the scope and on the terms specified in the description of the relevant course.
offer.s2_2: Access to free courses is provided after registration on the Platform without charge.
offer.s2_3: Access to paid courses is provided after the Customer has made full payment in accordance with the selected plan.
offer.s2_4: The list of courses, their descriptions, and prices are published in the Platform catalog and may be changed by the Provider.
offer.s3_1: Acceptance of this Offer is constituted by the Customer's actions of registering on the Platform and/or paying for paid educational services.
offer.s3_2: Acceptance of the Offer means the Customer's full and unconditional acceptance of the terms of this Offer and is equivalent to the conclusion of a contract for the provision of educational services.
offer.s3_3: The moment of acceptance is the moment of payment confirmation for a paid service or the completion of registration for a free course.
offer.s4_1: The cost of educational services is indicated in Russian rubles (RUB) and is published on each course page in the Platform catalog.
offer.s4_2: Payment is made through integrated payment systems, the list of which is available on the Platform.
offer.s4_3: The cost of services includes all applicable taxes, including VAT, unless otherwise specified in the course description.
offer.s4_4: The Provider has the right to change the cost of services by notifying the Customer at least 5 (five) calendar days before the changes take effect.
offer.s4_5: Price changes do not apply to services already paid for by the Customer.
offer.s5_1: Access to the course is provided within 24 hours of payment confirmation or immediately after registration (for free courses).
offer.s5_2: Educational services are provided in electronic form through the Platform's web interface.
offer.s5_3: Access to course materials is provided for an unlimited period, unless otherwise specified in the course description.
offer.s5_4: The Provider has the right to update and supplement the content of courses without degrading their quality.
offer.s6_1: Refunds are processed in accordance with the Law of the Russian Federation "On Protection of Consumers' Rights" and this Offer.
offer.s6_2: In the case of distance selling, the Customer has the right to refuse the service within 14 calendar days from the date of payment (Article 26.1 of the Consumer Protection Law).
offer.s6_3: After starting a course, refunds are made proportionally to the unused portion of the service.
offer.s6_4: Detailed refund conditions are set out in the Refund Policy, which is an integral part of this Offer.
offer.s6_5: Refunds are made using the same method by which payment was made, within 10 business days.
offer.s7_1: The Platform and educational services are provided "as is." The Provider does not guarantee that the Customer will achieve specific learning outcomes.
offer.s7_2: The aggregate liability of the Provider to the Customer is limited to the amount actually paid by the Customer for the relevant service.
offer.s7_3: The Provider is not liable for temporary technical failures in the operation of the Platform that are not caused by its fault.
offer.s7_4: The parties shall be released from liability for failure to perform obligations in the event of force majeure circumstances.
refund.s1_1: This Refund Policy (hereinafter — "Policy") is developed in accordance with the Law of the Russian Federation No. 2300-1 of February 7, 1992 "On Protection of Consumers' Rights" and the Decree of the Government of the Russian Federation No. 1724 of December 31, 2020 "On Approval of the Rules for the Sale of Goods under a Retail Sale and Purchase Agreement."
refund.s1_2: The Policy is an integral part of the Public Offer of the Maestria educational platform and applies to all paid services provided by the Operator.
refund.s1_3: Free courses and courses provided via promotional codes without monetary payment are not eligible for refunds.
refund.s2_1: In accordance with Article 26.1 of the Consumer Protection Law, in the case of distance selling, the Customer has the right to refuse the service at any time before it begins, and after it begins — within 14 calendar days without giving reasons.
refund.s2_2: The right to refuse the service within 14 calendar days does not apply when the Customer has consented to the commencement of the service before this period has expired and has voluntarily started the course.
refund.s2_3: If the service is refused before it begins, a full refund is issued within 10 business days from the date the Customer's request is received.
refund.s2_4: The costs of processing the refund are borne by the Provider.
refund.s3_1: If the Customer has started the course and completed less than 25% of the learning materials, a refund of 75% of the course price is issued.
refund.s3_2: If between 25% and 50% of the learning materials have been completed, a refund of 50% of the course price is issued.
refund.s3_3: If the Customer has completed more than 50% of the learning materials, no refund is issued, except in cases provided for by legislation.
refund.s3_4: The course completion percentage is determined based on data from the Platform's automatic progress tracking system.
refund.s4_1: To request a refund, the Customer must send a written statement to maksimqwe42@mail.ru specifying: full name, email address, course title, date of payment, reason for the refund, and attaching proof of payment.
refund.s4_2: The request is reviewed within 5 business days. The Customer is notified of the results by email.
refund.s4_3: Refunds are made using the same method by which payment was made, within 10 business days from the date of the positive decision.
refund.s4_4: When issuing a refund, the Provider has the right to deduct the cost of services actually rendered in proportion to the amount of completed material.
refund.s5_1: Refunds are not issued in the following cases:
refund.s5_1a: Violation of the User Agreement or Platform Rules by the Customer;
refund.s5_1b: Blocking of the Customer's account for violation of Platform Rules;
refund.s5_1c: Completion of more than 50% of the course learning materials;
refund.s5_1d: More than 14 calendar days have passed since the service began without valid reasons.
eduinfo.disclaimer: The information is published in accordance with the requirements of Federal Law No. 273-FZ of December 29, 2012 "On Education in the Russian Federation." The information may be updated without prior notice.
eduinfo.s2_1: The organization is registered and operates in accordance with the legislation of the Russian Federation.
eduinfo.s2_2: The organization's activities are carried out on the basis of founding documents that comply with the requirements of current legislation.
eduinfo.s2_3: The full text of the founding documents is provided upon request of the Customer or authorized government bodies.
eduinfo.s3_1: The Maestria Platform provides supplementary educational services in accordance with Federal Law No. 273-FZ "On Education in the Russian Federation."
eduinfo.s3_2: The courses are aimed at professional development, skill enhancement, and personal enrichment of Users.
eduinfo.s3_3: Upon completion of courses, Users are issued certificates of the established form, which are not state-issued educational documents.
eduinfo.s3_4: The content of educational programs complies with professional standards and labor market requirements.
eduinfo.s3_5: The organization continuously improves the quality of educational services and updates its training programs.
eduinfo.s4_1: The Platform's teaching staff possesses the qualifications and experience necessary for the quality provision of educational services.
eduinfo.s4_2: Instructors undergo regular assessment and professional development.
eduinfo.s4_3: The Platform's organizational structure includes content development, technical support, and quality control departments.
eduinfo.s5_1: The Platform operates on a cloud infrastructure that ensures high availability and reliability of services.
eduinfo.s5_2: A modern web platform with video hosting support, interactive assignments, and a testing system is used for the educational process.
eduinfo.s5_3: The Platform ensures the accessibility of educational materials for persons with disabilities in accordance with legislation.
eduinfo.s6_1: The financial support of the organization's activities is provided through payment for educational services by Users and investment funds.
eduinfo.s6_2: Pricing is transparent; there are no hidden fees or commissions. Financial reports are provided upon request of authorized bodies in accordance with legislation.
eduinfo.s7_1: Enrollment in courses is on a first-come, first-served basis without entrance examinations.
eduinfo.s7_2: To enroll, the User must register on the Platform and, if necessary, pay for the course.
eduinfo.s7_3: Access to free courses is provided immediately after registration is completed.
rules.s1_1: These Rules (hereinafter — "Rules") are developed in accordance with Federal Law No. 149-FZ of July 27, 2006 "On Information, Information Technologies, and Information Protection" and govern the procedure for using the Maestria educational platform.
rules.s1_2: The Rules are mandatory for all Platform Users, including unregistered visitors.
rules.s1_3: Violation of the Rules may result in restriction of access to the Platform, up to and including account blocking.
rules.s2_1: Each User may have only one account. Creating multiple accounts is prohibited.
rules.s2_2: During registration, the User is obliged to provide accurate and complete information. The User is responsible for the accuracy of the information provided.
rules.s2_3: Use of the Platform is permitted for persons aged 14 and older. Persons under 14 may use the Platform only with the consent of their parents or legal guardians.
rules.s2_4: The User is responsible for the security of their account and must not share their login and password with third parties.
rules.s3_1: The User is obliged to respect other participants in the educational process and refrain from insults, threats, and discriminatory statements.
rules.s3_2: Posting spam, flooding, advertising, and messages unrelated to the educational process is prohibited.
rules.s3_3: Discussions in comments and forums must be constructive and relevant to the course topic.
rules.s3_4: The Platform supports academic integrity. Cheating, plagiarism, and the use of unauthorized materials when completing assignments are prohibited.
rules.s4_1: User content created and published on the Platform (task solutions, comments, articles) is distributed under the Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike 4.0 International (CC BY-SA 4.0) license.
rules.s4_2: The User guarantees that the content they publish does not infringe the copyright of third parties and does not contain plagiarism.
rules.s4_3: Posting content that violates the legislation of the Russian Federation, including information and education laws, is prohibited.
rules.s4_4: The User is responsible for the content they publish.
rules.s5_1: The following content is prohibited on the Platform:
rules.s5_1a: Extremist materials (in accordance with Federal Law No. 114-FZ "On Countering Extremist Activity");
rules.s5_1b: Pornographic materials and materials exploiting minors;
rules.s5_1c: Materials promoting violence, cruelty, drug abuse, substance abuse, alcoholism, and suicide;
rules.s5_1d: Materials infringing the intellectual property rights of third parties;
rules.s5_1e: Personal data of third parties without their consent;
rules.s5_1g: Malicious software and materials capable of disrupting the Platform's operation.
rules.s6_1: The Platform Administration moderates user content and has the right to delete or edit materials that violate the Rules.
rules.s6_2: The moderation system includes: warning the User, temporary restriction of access, and permanent account blocking for serious violations.
rules.s6_3: The User has the right to appeal a moderation decision by sending a request to maksimqwe42@mail.ru.
rules.s6_4: Complaints are reviewed within 10 business days from the date of receipt.
rules.s7_1: All Platform materials (courses, lessons, tests, images, source code) are the intellectual property of Maestro7IT or the course authors.
rules.s7_2: User content is distributed under the CC BY-SA 4.0 license with mandatory attribution.
rules.s7_3: Upon discovery of intellectual property rights violations, the User may file a complaint in accordance with the requirements of Federal Law No. 149-FZ.
rules.s8_1: The Administration reserves the right to make changes to these Rules. The current version is always available on the Platform page.
rules.s8_2: For any questions related to the Rules, you may contact: maksimqwe42@mail.ru.
license.s1_1: User content created and published on the Maestria educational platform is distributed under the Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike 4.0 International (CC BY-SA 4.0) license.
license.s1_2: This License Agreement defines the terms of use of user content published on the Platform by third parties.
license.s1_3: The CC BY-SA 4.0 license is an open license developed by Creative Commons and is recognized in jurisdictions worldwide.
license.s2_1: The license grants the right to Share — copy and distribute the material in any medium and format.
license.s2_2: The license grants the right to Adapt — remix, transform, and create new materials based on the licensed material.
license.s2_3: The Licensee may use the material for any purpose, including commercial purposes, provided the conditions of the license are met.
license.s3_1: When using user content, Attribution is mandatory — providing appropriate credit, a link to the license, and indicating whether changes were made.
license.s3_2: When creating derivative materials, they must be distributed under the same CC BY-SA 4.0 license (ShareAlike).
license.s3_3: The application of additional legal restrictions or technological measures that limit the rights granted by the license is prohibited.
license.s3_4: Attribution requirements: name of the author, title of the material, link to the material, link to the CC BY-SA 4.0 license.
license.s4_1: This license does not override or limit fair use rights and other rights recognized under copyright law.
license.s4_2: Publicity, privacy, and moral rights may limit the use of the material in certain cases.
license.s5_1: The licensed material is provided "as is," without any warranties, express or implied.
license.s5_2: The Licensor does not warrant the material's suitability for any particular purpose, its accuracy, completeness, or absence of third-party rights infringements.
license.s5_3: This license does not create any warranty obligations on the part of the Licensor.
license.s6_1: The Licensor is not liable to the Licensee for any losses, damages, or expenses arising from the use of the licensed material.
license.s6_2: The Licensor's liability, if any, is limited to the maximum extent permitted by applicable law.
license.s7_1: This license and the rights granted under it automatically terminate upon the Licensee's breach of the license conditions.
license.s7_2: Licenses of persons who received derivative materials or copies from the Licensee are not terminated, provided they comply with the license conditions.
age.s1_1: This Age Rating Policy is developed in accordance with Federal Law No. 436-FZ of December 29, 2010 "On Protection of Children from Information Harmful to Their Health and Development."
age.s1_2: The Policy determines the procedure for labeling educational content on the Maestria Platform by age category.
age.s1_3: The purpose of the Policy is to ensure the information security of minor users of the Platform.
age.s2_1: The following age category system is applied on the Platform:
age.s2_1a: Content intended for persons of any age. Does not contain information harmful to the health and development of children.
age.s2_1b: Content intended for children aged 6 and older. May contain minor scenes that do not harm the health and development of children.
age.s2_1c: Content intended for persons aged 12 and older. May contain episodic depictions of cruelty without detailed portrayal.
age.s2_1d: Content intended for persons aged 16 and older. May contain information that does not harm health and development but requires a certain level of maturity for perception.
age.s2_1e: Content intended exclusively for adults (18+). Contains information prohibited for children in accordance with legislation.
age.s2_2: The overwhelming majority of courses on the Platform carry an age rating of 6+ or 12+.
age.s3_1: The age rating is displayed on each course page before the start of learning.
age.s3_2: The course content description includes information about possible content requiring age restrictions.
age.s3_3: For courses rated 6+ and 12+, recommendations for parents on supervising minors' learning are provided.
age.s4_1: Registration on the Platform requires the User to confirm their age. Persons under 14 may use the Platform only with the consent of their parents or legal guardians.
age.s4_2: Access to content rated 16+ and 18+ requires age verification.
age.s4_3: The Platform provides technical means to restrict minors' access to content that is not appropriate for their age.
age.s4_4: If content prohibited for distribution to children is identified, the Administration undertakes to promptly take measures to remove it or restrict access.
age.s5_1: Parents and legal guardians are responsible for supervising minors' use of the Platform.
age.s5_2: The Platform provides tools and recommendations for parental control, including age ratings and content filtering.
age.s5_3: If content does not match its age rating, Users may report it to the Administration via the feedback form or at maksimqwe42@mail.ru.
cookie.s1_1: This Cookie Policy (hereinafter — "Policy") is developed in accordance with Federal Law No. 152-FZ of July 27, 2006 "On Personal Data" and No. 149-FZ "On Information, Information Technologies, and Information Protection."
cookie.s1_2: Cookies are small text files placed on the User's device when visiting websites, used to store preferences and data about interactions with the site.
cookie.s1_3: By using the Maestria Platform, you confirm your consent to the use of cookies in accordance with this Policy.
cookie.s2_1: The Maestria Platform uses the following types of cookies:
cookie.s2_1a: ensure the Platform's functionality, User authentication, and session security. Without these cookies, the Platform cannot function correctly.
cookie.s2_1b: remember the User's settings (selected interface language, theme, display preferences) and provide a personalized experience.
cookie.s2_1c: allow the collection of anonymized usage statistics, analysis of User behavior, and improvement of service quality.
cookie.s2_1d: used to display relevant advertising and track the effectiveness of advertising campaigns. Used only with the User's consent.
cookie.s3_1: We use cookies for the following purposes:
cookie.s3_1a: Authentication and maintenance of the User's active session on the Platform;
cookie.s3_1b: Session management and prevention of unauthorized access to the account;
cookie.s3_1c: Saving user preferences (language, theme, display settings);
cookie.s3_1d: Collection of aggregated analytical information to improve the Platform's operation;
cookie.s3_1e: Ensuring security and protection against automated attacks (DDoS, bots);
cookie.s3_1f: Personalization of recommendations and the user interface.
cookie.s4_1: The User can manage cookies through their browser settings: view, delete, and block individual cookies.
cookie.s4_2: Disabling or blocking cookies may result in limited functionality of the Platform, including the inability to authenticate and save settings.
cookie.s4_3: The Platform provides a cookie consent mechanism on the first visit, as well as the ability to change settings at any time.
cookie.s5_1: The Platform may use third-party cookies from analytics services (e.g., Yandex.Metrica) to collect statistical data.
cookie.s5_2: Payment systems used for course payments may set their own cookies to ensure transaction security.
cookie.s5_3: Embedded video players (VK Video, Rutube) may use cookies for correct video content display.
cookie.s6_1: The Administration reserves the right to update this Policy. The current version is always available on the Platform page.
cookie.s6_2: For questions related to the use of cookies, you may contact: maksimqwe42@mail.ru.

home.heroDescription: Study programming, web development, game creation, and Data Science with top instructors at your own pace
home.directionsSubtitle: Choose a path that interests you
home.whyMaestriaSubtitle: We create the best online learning experience
home.startNowSubtitle: Join thousands of students on the Maestria platform
about.missionDesc: We believe quality education should be accessible to everyone. Our platform brings together top instructors and modern technology to create a unique programming learning experience.
about.missionBody1: Maestria is not just an educational platform — it's an entire ecosystem created by Maestro7IT with the goal of democratizing access to quality programming education. We believe that every person, regardless of age, location, or financial situation, deserves the opportunity to master an in-demand profession in information technology. Our platform combines the best online learning methodologies with an individual approach to each student, creating a unique environment for development.
about.missionBody2: At the heart of Maestria lies integration with the MentorHUB educational community, which enables an effective mentoring and mutual support system between instructors and students. MentorHUB is a space where experienced mentors share their knowledge, help overcome learning challenges, and guide students on their path to professional growth. This fosters a living community of like-minded individuals united by a common goal — to become professionals in the world of programming.
about.missionBody3: We carefully design each course based on real labor market needs and feedback from leading IT companies. Our step-by-step learning system allows progression from simple topics to complex ones, reinforcing each knowledge block with practical assignments and projects. Students don't just watch lectures — they write code, solve problems, and create their own projects, building a portfolio that will help them in employment. Each course concludes with certification confirming acquired competencies.
about.missionBody4: Our ultimate goal is to become a bridge between the desire to learn and a real career in the IT industry. We strive to ensure that every Maestria graduate not only gains knowledge but also finds confidence in their abilities and the capacity to solve real professional challenges. We continuously improve the platform, add new learning directions, and expand our team of instructors to remain at the cutting edge of educational technology.
about.leadershipDesc: The person behind the creation of the platform
about.directorBody1: Duplei Maksim Igorevich is the founder and driving force behind Maestro7IT, under which the Maestria educational platform was created. With a deep understanding of both the technical and educational spheres, Maksim Igorevich set himself an ambitious goal — to create a platform that would become a new quality standard in online programming education.
about.directorBody2: It is his vision that underlies Maestria's unique learning methodology, combining interactive lessons, a mentoring system through MentorHUB, and a practice-oriented approach. Maksim personally oversees the development of educational programs and the selection of instructors, ensuring that each course meets the platform's high standards.
about.directorBody3: Under Maksim Igorevich's leadership, Maestro7IT has grown from a small educational project into a full-fledged ecosystem uniting thousands of students and dozens of experienced instructors. His credo — education should be not only high-quality but truly accessible — and this principle is embodied in every aspect of the Maestria platform.
about.whyChooseDesc: Six reasons to entrust us with your education
about.advInteractiveDesc: Our courses are not boring video lectures, but a fully interactive experience. You write code directly in the browser, instantly see the results of your actions, and receive automated feedback from the system. This approach allows for faster material absorption and turns learning into an engaging, game-like process.
about.advStepByStepDesc: Each course is broken into logical steps and modules, arranged from simple to complex. You always know what stage you're at, what's already completed, and what lies ahead. The step-by-step structure allows you to effectively plan your learning time without feeling overwhelmed by the volume of information.
about.advTeachersDesc: All our instructors are practicing specialists with years of experience at leading IT companies. They not only possess deep subject knowledge but also know how to explain complex concepts clearly. Thanks to the MentorHUB community, every student receives personal mentoring and support.
about.advCertificatesDesc: Upon completing each course, you receive a personalized certificate confirming your acquired competencies and skills. Our certificates are recognized by partner companies and can be a valuable addition to your resume when applying for jobs. The certificate remains in your digital portfolio on the platform forever.
about.advSBPDesc: We accept payments through the Fast Payment System (SBP), which guarantees instant fund transfers with no commissions. This is the most convenient and secure way to pay for courses within the Russian Federation. Other payment methods are also available for your convenience.
about.adv247Desc: The platform operates around the clock, every day of the week. Learn at any convenient time, from anywhere in the world — all you need is internet access. All course materials are available immediately after payment, and you can return to them an unlimited number of times. Your progress is saved automatically on any device.
about.directionsDesc: Choose a path that opens doors to the IT world
about.dirPython: Python is one of the most in-demand programming languages in the world. In our courses, you'll master Python syntax, learn to work with libraries, create automation scripts, and build server applications. Python is ideal both as a first language and for in-depth study of specialized areas, including web development and automation.
about.dirWeb: Learn to create modern web applications from scratch. Our courses cover HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and popular frameworks and libraries. You'll master responsive design, interactive interfaces, server API integration, and project deployment. Web development is the foundation of the digital economy and one of the most in-demand professions.
about.dirRoblox: Create your own games in Roblox Studio! This direction is ideal for young developers and anyone who wants to dive into game development. You'll learn to program in Lua, create 3D worlds, set up game mechanics, and publish your projects. Roblox is an excellent starting point for future game developers.
about.dirCpp: Powerful systems programming languages that open the path to developing high-performance applications, game engines, and software. Our C++ and C# courses provide a deep understanding of memory principles, object-oriented programming, and algorithms necessary for a serious technical career.
about.dirDS: Data Science is one of the fastest-growing areas in IT. Learn to analyze data, build predictive models, and visualize results using Python, Pandas, NumPy, and machine learning libraries. Our courses provide practical skills working with real datasets and preparing reports demanded in business.
about.dirMobile: Mobile applications surround us everywhere, and the demand for mobile developers continues to grow. Learn to create applications for iOS and Android using modern frameworks and tools. You'll master interface design, API integration, data storage, and publishing applications to the App Store and Google Play.
about.contactDesc: We're always happy to answer your questions
about.platformDesc: We believe that quality education in programming should be accessible to everyone. Maestria is a place where knowledge turns into skills, and skills into a successful IT career.
